title: Why did Manga Entertainment get the movie rights
display_title: Why did Manga Entertainment get the movie rights
wikitext:
[]: Why did Manga Entertainment get the movie rights?             Quote Post by V » Thu Apr 03, 2008 9:32 pm Quick Question: does anyone know off the top of their head why Manga Entertainment got the rights to dub the movies, instead of ADV? (Resulting in voice changes like Black Hyuga) Simply a matter of losing a bidding war? or what?

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Thu Apr 03, 2008 9:42 pm I don't know but maybe it's because ADV has too much on its plate and gives some of the work to Gainax or like you said, it could be a bidding war.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Thu Apr 03, 2008 9:58 pm just to make sure I'm talking about End of Eva, not Rebuild

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Fri Apr 04, 2008 11:13 am Before manga got the movies I asked ADV about this, they said that after talking it over with Gainax it was decided that they wouldnt be liscensing the movies. They didnt go into specifics why though.

[]:             Quote Post by mrpanda » Fri Apr 04, 2008 11:23 am Black Hyuga was way cooler anyway, ADV probubly had to much on their plate which meant they were finantially commited to other projects therefore lost the bidding war. (i combined the theorys lol)

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Fri Apr 04, 2008 12:32 pm Specifics was $$$. Gainax wanted too much money for it so ADV balked. Manga on the other hand ponied up the dough and got the movies.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Fri Apr 04, 2008 2:33 pm Hunter21 wrote: Specifics was $$$. Gainax wanted too much money for it so ADV balked. Manga on the other hand ponied up the dough and got the movies.

[]:             Quote Post by Semisubtle » Fri Apr 04, 2008 2:58 pm Well, ADV's a larger company, right? They'd have their money distributed to more products at once, and maybe they just couldn't take it on at that time, so Manga did instead.

[]:             Quote Post by Grand Duke of Yashima » Fri Apr 04, 2008 4:27 pm At the time ADV was negotiating with GAINAX for the rights to EoE, Greenfield said that the price they wanted for EoE was the equivelant of ADV's entire anime licensing budget for that year. At the time, he thought it was a bad deal and walked away from it, so Manga snapped it up. Later on they had a lot of regrets as it sold quite well (and probably a heck of a lot better than the second-rate titles that ADV usually gets). I suspect one reason why we haven't heard about a R1 license for the Rebulid yet is that GAINAX's price tag is again very high and they are also getting more involved in HOW the anime is marketed here in the US. I've heard some rumors that the TTGL license deal with ADV was held up because GAINAX is pushing to have it on CN or another broadcast venue prior to DVD release. If they're taking that same tack with Rebuild, that might be why we don't have a license announcement yet.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sun Apr 06, 2008 9:30 am well that answers that, thank you I was wondering if it was due to either movies having some wacky different distribution scheme, or if ADV was having particular money problems at the time, but it does seem to just be simply due to: "bidding war", and Gainax asked a lot of money for the movie due to it being a hit alrighty

[]:             Quote Post by Grand Duke of Yashima » Wed Apr 09, 2008 1:35 am Don't forget that the very first anime convention in the US was AnimeCon '91 in San Jose, organized and underwritten by GAINAX. They have a better perception of the American anime market than most would give them credit for.

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Wed Apr 09, 2008 2:55 pm The problem with that... Is TTGL won't be released over here either until they're good and ready to realease it over there. Rebuild and TTGL are the two highest things on my 'sell soul to corporation for pittance to buy' list. And when they do come out, they'll probably take one smegger of a margin off them too. Especially over here when anime is irritatingly overpriced.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Apr 09, 2008 5:21 pm Dartz wrote: Especially over here when anime is irritatingly overpriced.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Apr 09, 2008 5:34 pm Ornette wrote: A large part of this reason is because the Japanese Studio

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Wed Apr 09, 2008 5:41 pm I recall hearing (from MDWigs, IIRC) that there was some resentment and "bad blood' on the part of Gainax towards ADV over some aspects of their original localization of NGE, in particular over how Anno was credited in the OP.

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Wed Apr 09, 2008 5:45 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Uh, no. In the UK, what in the US might be a $20 DVD will retail for £20 (and probably in Eire, where Dartz seems to reside, if I read rightly, for €30).

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Apr 09, 2008 6:29 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Uh, no. In the UK, what in the US might be a $20 DVD will retail for £20 (and probably in Eire, where Dartz seems to reside, if I read rightly, for €30).

[]:             Quote Post by RandomStuff » Mon Apr 14, 2008 9:40 pm We can only wish they hadn't. I spent an hour with my friends figuring out what the hell was with the sound effects.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Tue Apr 15, 2008 4:39 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Uh, no. In the UK, what in the US might be a $20 DVD will retail for £20 (and probably in Eire, where Dartz seems to reside, if I read rightly, for €30).

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Apr 15, 2008 5:11 pm No, it's just part of the general £1 buying here what $1 buys over there for everything. In this particular case, the total market for an R2/PAL/English DVD will be smaller than for an R1/NTSC, but incur all the re-encode and remastering costs, so the unit cost goes up; that's in addition to the expectation of paying higher prices. I don't have precise figures, but I have been told by those who should know for other geek hobby items, the UK is a smaller market than the Bay Area alone. By comparison, I imported the Princess Tutu complete box set for under £18 as a present to myself this Yuletide just past.
summary:
[]: Why did Manga Entertainment get the movie rights?             Quote Post by V » Thu Apr 03, 2008 9:32 pm Quick Question: does anyone know off the top of their head why Manga Entertainment got the rights to dub the movies, instead of ADV? (Resulting in voice changes like Black Hyuga) Simply a matter of losing a bidding war? or what?

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 &raq
